mission.title: vllm: Phi-2 model broken (AttributeError in PhiConfig)
mission.description: Fix for the Phi-2 model failing to load in vLLM due to AttributeError: 'PhiConfig' object has no attribute 'layer_norm_epsilon' (should be 'layer_norm_eps') and missing 'rotary_dim' attribute. This affected vLLM v0.2.x with newer versions of the transformers library. Reported by 29+ users.

steps.title: Identify the issue
steps.description:
When loading the Phi-2 model (microsoft/phi-2) in vLLM, you may see one of these errors:
```python
AttributeError: 'PhiConfig' object has no attribute 'layer_norm_epsilon'. Did you mean: 'layer_norm_eps'?
```
or:
```python
AttributeError: 'PhiConfig' object has no attribute 'rotary_dim'
```
This happens because the transformers library updated the Phi config attribute names, but older vLLM versions referenced the old names.

steps.title: Check your vLLM and transformers versions
steps.description:
Verify your installed versions:
```bash
pip show vllm | grep Version
pip show transformers | grep Version
```
This issue occurs with vLLM v0.2.x and transformers >= 4.36.0 where the PhiConfig attribute names changed.

steps.title: Apply the fix
steps.description:
Upgrade vLLM to a version that handles the updated PhiConfig attributes:
```bash
pip install vllm --upgrade
```
The fix in vLLM updated the model executor code (`phi_1_5.py`) to use the correct config attribute names (`layer_norm_eps` instead of `layer_norm_epsilon`, and `partial_rotary_factor` instead of `rotary_dim`).

steps.title: Verify Phi-2 loads correctly
steps.description:
Test that Phi-2 loads and runs without errors:
```bash
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model microsoft/phi-2
```
Then test inference:
```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/v1/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "microsoft/phi-2", "prompt": "def hello_world():", "max_tokens": 50}'
```
You should receive a valid completion without any AttributeError.

resolution.summary: The Phi-2 model broke in vLLM due to a mismatch between the PhiConfig attribute names used in vLLM's model executor and the updated attribute names in the transformers library. Specifically, 'layer_norm_epsilon' was renamed to 'layer_norm_eps' and 'rotary_dim' was replaced with 'partial_rotary_factor'. The fix updated vLLM's Phi model code to use the correct attribute names.
codeSnippets:
# Error: AttributeError: 'PhiConfig' object has no attribute 'layer_norm_epsilon'
# Fix: Use 'layer_norm_eps' instead of 'layer_norm_epsilon'
# Fix: Use 'partial_rotary_factor' instead of 'rotary_dim'

# Upgrade vLLM to fix the issue
pip install vllm --upgrade

prerequisites.description: Python 3.8+, pip, a CUDA-capable GPU, and a working vLLM installation (pip install vllm).
